crime.value: Serial murders disguised as suicides - 6 women killed and given charm bracelets spelling out the killer's name
crime.quote: He had murdered 6 women then gave them charm bracelets with their initials which spell out his name

victim.value: Six women including Tom's wife Tara, targeted to spell out 'THOMAS' with their initials on charm bracelets
victim.quote: Tara Jackson – T Hannah Fielding - H Olivia Johnson - O Melanie Jones - M Alice Hybri - A Susie Jack – S

closed_world.value: Tom Jackson's fractured psyche - the investigation is contained within his mental illness and dual personality
closed_world.quote: he was searching for a non existent killer, but the notes revealed that there indeed was a murderer

culprit_and_motive.value: Tom Jackson/Thomas Morgan (alternate personality) driven by schizophrenia and grief over his wife's death
culprit_and_motive.quote: It was Thomas Jackson; you may say it was his alternate personality that he named Thomas Morgan

resolution.value: Tom discovers through his own investigation notes that he is the killer, then commits suicide by his wife's grave
resolution.quote: A note book was found in his hand, this note book revealed everything he had learnt about the cases

investigator.value: Tom Jackson, journalist investigating his own crimes without realizing he committed them
investigator.quote: Tom worked on the case of his wife's suicide alongside 4 other suicides before, and 1 after

suspect_architecture.value: Single perpetrator with split personality - Tom Jackson/Thomas Morgan, his alternate identity. No traditional suspect pool as the protagonist is both investigator and unknowing culprit.
suspect_architecture.quote: It was Thomas Jackson; you may say it was his alternate personality that he named Thomas Morgan

red_herring.value: The search for an external serial killer when the murders were committed by the investigator's alternate personality during schizophrenic episodes.
red_herring.quote: some people thought he was searching for a non existent killer, but the notes revealed that there indeed was a murderer

clue_fairness.value: Charm bracelets with initials spelling 'THOMAS' - clue is present but significance not revealed until the solution.
clue_fairness.quote: then gave them charm bracelets with their initials which spell out his name

social_world.value: Journalism hierarchy with Tom as 'top journalist' reporting to boss Julia; institutional care system that failed Tom in childhood.
social_world.quote: as my top journalist, I would like you to take on the story

alibi.value: Tom's work schedule provides unconscious alibi - wife's suicide occurred 'while he was at work', but this may be fabricated memory.
alibi.quote: she committed suicide in the early hours of the morning while he was at work

reveal_mechanic.value: Notebook found in Tom's dead hand containing the acrostic pattern of victims' initials spelling his name, demonstrating the systematic nature.
reveal_mechanic.quote: A note book was found in his hand, this note book revealed everything

media_and_audience.value: The story is framed as published journalism investigation, with Tom working for The Times newspaper covering the suicide cases.
media_and_audience.quote: becoming a journalist for The Times newspaper. Tom worked on the case of his wife's suicide

investigator_wound.value: Tom's schizophrenia and trauma from his wife's suicide, driving obsessive investigation behavior that masks his own dissociative episodes.
investigator_wound.quote: On the 8th of March 2011, Tom tragically lost his wife, she committed suicide
